sections.title: Introduction
sections.content:
The objectives of this University will be better achieved where there is optimum student welfare. It is the policy of the University therefore, to create an environment that affords the students maximum opportunity to derive from their education the highest possible quality of personal fulfilment, professional attainment, social responsibility and service to the community.

To this end, the University shall:
list: i. Encourage responsible student union activities, clubs and associations. At all times student shall conduct themselves decorously in a manner worthy of ladies or gentlemen.

ii. Maintain open communication among all segments of the University community through the establishment of clear and reliable channels for free exchange of ideas and opinions and for the dissemination of accurate and authorized information.

iii. Involve students in the management of their own affairs and University affairs wherever and whenever practicable so far as these directly affect their well-being. This is in the belief that to make a student responsible, he/she shall be given responsibility to carry out in order to appreciate the difficulties of decision-making and, more importantly, decision-implementation.

sections.title: Student Union Government
sections.content: The Federal government of Nigeria has a policy that prohibits the formation of Student Unions in newly established universities for the first five years. This policy is intended to allow the new institutions to stabilize and focus on academic and administrative development without the potential disruption that union activities might cause.

sections.title: Open Communication
sections.content:
The Vice-Chancellor shall hold regular dialogue with students and address the students at least once a session.

The University shall organize an orientation programme for fresh students to familiarize them with the University, giving them favourable first impression and acquainting the new students with the traditions of the University.

There shall be a University newspaper run primarily by students, with technical advice from Senior Staff. The primary purpose of this is threefold:
list: i. Inform the University of New and Projected Developments as well as decisions and activities of the various student groups and University Community.
ii. Provide avenue for expression of students' views on issues that affect them; and,
iii. To serve as a training ground for student journalists.

sections.title: Clubs and Associations
sections.content:
The Dean, Student Affairs, on behalf of Senate and Council, shall approve the registration of clubs and associations. Only such approval and registration may entitle a club or an association to be operated by students on campus.

Any association applying for approval and registration shall supply information about its membership, their full names, residential addresses, email addresses and the departments of key officers as well as the names of their staff advisers and patrons. The application form shall be returned, with a copy of the club or association's constitution. There shall be an annual registration which entitles the clubs or associations to operate for only the academic year during which it is registered.
Students are encouraged to join University approved clubs and associations which serve social, academic and cultural purposes as well as train or improve the quality of leadership ability of students, teach good community relationship and help students appreciate the problems of leadership and followership.

sections.title: Social and Cultural Activities
sections.intro:
Registered clubs/associations are encouraged to undertake a variety of activities in each academic session for the benefit of their members and the University community in particular and the outside world in general. In doing so, they must abide by the University regulations on Hall facilities. Permission must be obtained from the Dean, Student Affairs through the Director of Socials before any activity is undertaken.
Students who wish to organize social activities open to the public on campus shall comply with the following procedures:
list: I. Shall apply to the Registrar through the Dean of Student Affairs on prescribed forms obtained from the Student Affairs Office for permission to use University facilities.

ii. Permission must be obtained from the Hall Warden through the Hall Executive concerned, in the case where a Hall facility such as Common-Room will be used. Where a refectory is to be used, the operator of the refectory shall first be informed and clearance obtained from the Dean of Student Affairs at least one week before the social activity is scheduled to hold.

iii. Payment of the stipulated rent for hiring Hall facilities shall be paid at the Bursary and a receipt obtained at least three working days before the social activity is scheduled to hold. A copy of the receipt is tendered to the appropriate Hall Official (Social/Welfare/Sports Secretary) for records.

iv. No club, association or individual student is allowed to show films on campus for commercial purposes, i.e. where gate fees are charged except permission has been obtained in writing from the Dean of Student Affairs. However, academic films and others meant to promote student academic standing shall be encouraged provided no fees are charged.
sections.additional_info:
Students are, in their own interest, warned that neither the Hall Executives/Hall Warden nor the Dean, Student Affairs shall permit any part of the campus to be used for activities, social or otherwise, that are classified illegal by the laws of Nigeria. Thus, applications for use of Hall facilities are required to contain definite information regarding the nature of the activity for which the facility is required; e.g. lectures, symposia, debates or film shows. In the case of film shows, the application shall specify the title of film or films and their rating (P.G., X., XX, XXX, X- rated films etc). No films which promote violence, ethnicity, pornography, sectarianism or detract from our national objectives shall be permitted.

The Hall Warden, men/women of the Security Department and Dean, Student Affairs, or other officers acting on his behalf, must always be given access to sit in to observe the nature of the film in question. On no account must a different film be screened from that originally advertised. Severe disciplinary measures shall be taken against the student leaders of the club/society/association in such activity. Besides, the defaulting club/society/association may be suspended or proscribed.

It is the policy of the University to encourage students to organize themselves and join organizations which contribute to the academic, cultural, recreational and social life of the University and which enhance greater student interaction outside the classroom. These organizations sponsor various activities such as lectures, 'open house' parties, excursion trips, art exhibitions, drama etc.

All forms of secret cults or such other anti-social organizations by whatever names they may be called or known and which are illegal and not in the national interest are prohibited in the University.

sections.title: REGISTRATION OF STUDENT ORGANIZATIONS
sections.intro:
As a rule, the University does not encourage or recognize any student organization which, in its membership, discriminates on account of race, sex, religion etc.

Student organizations must meet the following conditions before being accorded University recognition:
list: i. Bona fide intent to pursue activities that will be consistent with the educational and social functions and established policies of the University as approved by the Governing Council and Senate of the Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi.
ii. Shall show proof of permanence in organization as evidenced by constitutions, rules and regulations, statement of financial support and sources commensurate with the activities to be undertaken.
iii. Shall show evidence that none of the existing student organizations meet the needs of the proposed organization(s).
iv. Membership of the proposed organization shall be demonstrated by a significant number of student members or prospective members.
v. Shall give the name(s) of staff- Patron(s)/Adviser(s)
vi. For this purpose, groups seeking recognition must submit the following to the Dean, Student Affairs Division:
a. Two copies of the proposed constitution or operative guidelines. The Dean, Student Affairs Division shall be notified of any future amendment within 14 days of the adoption of such amendments.
b. Names, matriculation numbers, course/department, addresses of the officers.
c. Signature of a full-time Senior Academic, Administrative or Technical Staff of the University who has agreed to serve as Adviser/Patron to the organization.
d. Certification by the Adviser/Patron that at least twenty full-time students will form the nucleus of the organization.
e. Statement of purpose and proposed programme of activities.
f. Affiliations, if any, to any external organization(s).
g. Statement of sponsorship from the Head of Department, Director of Centre, if the organization is to be sponsored by a Department, Centre or any other unit of the University.
h. Statement of proposed financial support and structure for the management of fund.

i. Such student organization may be dissolved by request of the organization itself or by action of the Dean, Student Affairs Division, where the organization fails to register for two consecutive Academic Sessions.
j. To re-establish an association that had been proscribed, the procedure shall be the same as establishing an association.
k. Quarterly reports of activities of student associations should be submitted to the Dean, Student Affairs Division.

l. Any student member of a registered association in the University found guilty of embezzling of association's funds shall be deemed to have committed an offence and shall be made to face the Disciplinary Committee for further appropriate punishment.

sections.title: Privileges and Recognition
sections.intro: Registered student organizations are entitled to the following privileges:
list: i. Use of University facilities subject to payment of appropriate charges in keeping with the scheduling and booking regulations and procedures governing the use of such facilities.
ii. The use of the University's name as part of their official titles
iii. May seek support from other affiliate organizations.

sections.title: General Regulations Governing Student Organizations
sections.content:
Registration of a student organization shall not be construed as an agreement for financial support by the University.

Only bona fide students of the University are eligible for membership of student organization(s).

All Student organizations must keep proper records of their finances in accordance with the guideline laid down from time to time by the Dean, students Affairs Division. All funds raised by student organizations by whatever means are subject to financial accountability.

sections.title: ACTIVITIES INVOLVING GUESTS FROM OFF-CAMPUS
sections.content:
In keeping with the University freedom of inquiry and pursuit of truth, students are allowed to organize activities featuring guests/artistes from outside the campus.

However, the University has an obligation to ensure that activities on the campus are conducted in a manner acceptable to the University community. Where a Head of State, a Governor, a Minister of State, a Head of Diplomatic Mission or some other important personality from outside the University is invited, the University must be informed through the Dean, Student Affairs Division, at least two weeks before the guest is expected to arrive on campus. This is to enable the University to coordinate the activity.

sections.title: CHANNEL OF COMMUNICATION
sections.content:
Any student who wishes to have audience with the Vice Chancellor on any matter should do so in writing through the Dean of Student Affairs Division stating clearly the reason(s) for the audience. Students' Associations should also do so through the Dean of Student Affairs Division.

Students wishing to visit or have audience with Non-teaching Departments/Units such as the Physical Planning and Maintenance Unit, Health Services, Bursary, etc must do so through the Dean, Student Affairs Division.

Nothing however, prevents students from reporting cases directly to the appropriate quarters, such as the Librarian, Chief Security Officer (CSO) or any other officer of the University if there is an emergency.

subsections.subtitle: i. Public Statement on University Affairs
subsections.content: Student organizations may hold private or public news conferences or submit news releases on student activities only when the contents of such releases have been presented to, and vetted by the University through the Dean, Student Affairs Division.

subsections.subtitle: ii. Student-Managed Magazine
subsections.content: The production/publication of obscene and lascivious pictures/cartoons and articles are absolutely prohibited. Students who engage in such publications/productions are liable to severe disciplinary action. Editors of such newspapers and magazines must avoid the production and publication of seditious or libelous articles or such publications which may provoke ethnic or sectarian violence or are capable of inciting one group of persons against another.

subsections.subtitle: iii. Bulletin Boards
subsections.content: Official notices are posted on the Notice Boards as guide/information for students and other members of the University community. Students shall be responsible for acquainting themselves with official notices. They are advised to avail themselves of information on Notice Boards from time-to-time. All notices to be placed on Notice Boards whether public or private, must bear the proper names and signature of those issuing them. They should also be properly dated. Approval for posting notices or removing them from the University Notice Boards must be obtained from the following:
list: a. Information and Public Relations Unit, Student Union – for posting on Student Union Notice Boards.
b. Hall Social Welfare Officer-for posting on Hall Notice Boards.
c. Head of Department for postings on departmental Notice Boards.
subsections.note: Students posting notice without proper approval shall be liable to disciplinary action. Provisions must be made on all notices for the signatures and names of approving bodies/persons.

subsections.subtitle: iv. Student Publications
subsections.content: The University maintains a tradition of encouraging various, free student press. Such publications on campus shall be guided by the following regulations:
list: a. Only registered student organizations may produce any publication or printed matter bearing the name of the University or purporting to emanate from it.
b. Printed matters published by students of the University may be sold on campus only if such materials are produced or sold by a registered student organization.
c. All student publications must carry the names of the organizations and members of the editorial board.

d. Any student organization, which publishes, sells, or distributes printed or otherwise reproduced materials on campus, is responsible for those materials, including their contents, and any libelous matters arising there from.

e. All student publications must state explicitly on the editorial page that the opinions expressed there are not those of the University or its student body as a whole. The name of the editor of the publication must appear in the publication.

f. All seditious, libelous and obscene publications, as well as publications capable of inciting one section of the students population against another or inciting the students against the University administration are prohibited.
g. A copy of every publication shall be deposited in the offices of the Vice Chancellor, Registrar, University Librarian and two copies in the Student Affairs Division.
